architecture.summary: MindMap organizes facts into evidence chains (sets of facts sharing the same subject) and integrates this structure into existing LLM reasoning frameworks. The model selects relevant evidence chains rather than independent facts during reasoning, with the chains fed as text context into CoT or Selection-Inference prompting pipelines.

biblio.title: MindMap: Constructing Evidence Chains for Multi-Step Reasoning in Large Language Models
biblio.venue: Proceedings of the AAAI Conference on Artificial Intelligence

claims.statement: MindMap can be integrated with existing reasoning frameworks, such as Chain-of-Thought (CoT) and Selection-Inference (SI), by letting the model select relevant evidence chains instead of independent facts.

baseline.name: CoT and SI without evidence chains

claims.statement: MindMap can significantly improve CoT and SI, especially in multi-step reasoning tasks.
Evidence chains organize related facts together to avoid missing important information in multi-step reasoning.
